title: Electrophysiology Protocol for Cortical Cultures
author: Yasmin Bar El
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.tqeemte

steps.title: For extracellular voltage recording and stimulation...
steps.instruction: For extracellular voltage recording and stimulation of cortical cultures use MEA (60MEA200/30iR-Ti-gr and 60MEA500/30iR-Ti-pr; Multichannel Systems), utilizing low noise pre-amplifier board (MEA- 1060-BC, amplifier, gain x1100; Multichannel Systems)； For recording simultaneously with calcium imaging: Place the amplifier under the microscope, connect the camera to the MC-card in the computer and to the stimulator (see instructions bellow, step 3) in order to record the time stamps of the camera and apply an external trigger for the camera to start recording (see MEA manual by Multichannel Systems for exact directions of cable connections).

steps.title: Use MC-RACK data acquisition software by...
steps.instruction: Use MC-RACK data acquisition software by Multichannel Systems to record the voltage signals and the triggers applied on the culture. For detailed use of the software, see MC-Rack manual.

steps.title: Perform electrical stimulation using a dedicated...
steps.instruction: Perform electrical stimulation using a dedicated four-channel stimulus generator (STG 2004; Multichannel Systems). Connect the stimulator to the computer, MEA amplifier, and camera. See manual in Multichannel Systems website.

steps.title: To send an external trigger to activate the camera
steps.instruction: To send an external trigger to activate the camera.

steps.title: To program an electrical stimulation protocol. For example
steps.instruction: To program an electrical stimulation protocol. For example: Rectangular and biphasic 400 µs-long current pulses of 25-35 µA (1.4-1.7 mC/cm

steps.title: at frequencies of 2-40 Hz
steps.instruction: at frequencies of 2-40 Hz. For more download the manuals from the Multichannel Systems website: https://www.multichannelsystems.com/downloads/documentation
